exemplar.title: Using trigonometry to find the distances of the Moon and Sun to Earth

detailedExemplarData.title: Using trigonometry to find the distances of the Moon and Sun to Earth

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear, purposeful logical development from curiosity through theory to application
Exceptionally well organized with a detailed table of contents and numbered sections
Consistent use of labeled figures, tables and cross-referencing enhances reader orientation
weaknesses: Minor repetition of definitions (e.g., arcseconds) in two sections
A few lengthy narrative sentences could be trimmed for greater conciseness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands (coherence and organization) clearly meet top-band descriptors and the structure is exemplary. Although conciseness falls slightly short (band 3) due to occasional repetition, the overall presentation is highly coherent, well organized and relevant, warranting the maximum score of 4.

strengths: Generally consistent and accurate mathematical notation (trigonometric functions, units, variables)
Effective use of multiple representations (algebraic derivations, annotated diagrams, Desmos graphs, data tables)
Logical, step-by-step layout of calculations supports reader comprehension
weaknesses: Minor notation slips (use of â=â instead of âââ in one heading, mismatched parentheses)
A few formatting glitches in formulas and wording duplications could be improved
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although small errors prevent flawless communication, the student demonstrates mostly consistent notation, effective multiple representations and clear logical structure. These elements align closely with the band-4 descriptors, justifying a score of 4.

strengths: Independent selection of an original astronomical question and use of personal telescope data
Application of ImageJ and Desmos scripting shows initiative
Narrative weaves in personal motivation and adaptation
weaknesses: Mathematical methods remain close to standard syllabus techniques without significant extension
Creativity and innovation are evident but not sustained at an exceptional level
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student shows clear evidence of independent thinking, a personal approach and some creativity (band 2). However, the investigation does not reach the outstanding originality or deep methodological innovation required for band 3, so the score remains 2.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on rounding errors, image-resolution limits and observational assumptions
Thoughtful comparison of calculation methods and error analyses
Clear evaluation of outcomes with suggestions for future extensions
weaknesses: Reflection lacks the deep, critical questioning and quantitative evaluation expected at the highest band
Analysis does not include statistical treatment of measurement uncertainty
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection and analysis are thoughtful and threaded throughout the conclusion (band 2), but they do not achieve the depth or critical rigour required for band 3. Thus, a score of 2 is appropriate.

strengths: Thorough and logically sound derivations with mostly error-free numerical work
Comprehensive and appropriate use of relevant mathematics (trigonometric proofs, Taylor series, error analysis)
Mathematics is at or slightly above SL level and well applied throughout
weaknesses: Minor calculation inconsistencies in tables (e.g., cosÎ¸ entries)
Opportunities to quantify errors (standard deviations, approximation impacts) are suggested but not implemented
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration employs relevant mathematics comprehensively and shows a thorough understanding aligned with band 5. Minor lapses prevent the award of band 6, so a score of 5 most accurately reflects the quality of mathematical use.

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanations flow logically and calculations are set out in a step-by-step fashion, enabling the reader to follow the mathematical argument.  Occasional formatting glitches (line-breaks in formulas, duplicated wording in the percentage-accuracy formula) slightly impede clarity, so the work fits **good but not flawless logical communication**, appropriate to band 3.

Derivations are logically sound, numerical work is largely error-free (pixel-to-degree conversions, use of 206 265 factor, percentage-error calculation), and conclusions match calculations.  Minor slips in presentation do not undermine a **thorough understanding and generally correct execution**, aligned with band 5.

The investigation integrates **algebraic derivations, annotated diagrams, Desmos graphs and data tables** to communicate ideas.  Each representation is relevant and supports understanding (e.g., graphs illustrate approximation accuracy; ImageJ screenshots justify pixel counts).  The range is good but not exceptionally varied or sophisticated, hence an effective rather than highly-effective use (band 3).

The exploration follows a **clear, purposeful line of reasoning**: it moves from a personal curiosity to theoretical derivations, then to formula building and finally to realâdata application and evaluation.  Each section logically anticipates the next (e.g., proof of the small-angle approximations precedes their use in deriving the angular-diameter formula).  Transitions between sections are smooth and there are no disruptive digressions, so the reader can follow the argument without difficulty.  Overall, the logical development is outstanding and fully supports the highest band descriptor.

Using ImageJ, scripting Desmos graphs and employing the MicroObservatory platform display **initiative and some creativity**.  The work proposes future extensions (angular area, parallax) which hint at further originality.  These features exceed basic expectations but are not exceptional or novel enough for band 3.

Critical analysis is evident in the comparison of two calculation methods, percentage-error tables, and discussion of why cosine requires a higher-order approximation.  The analysis is **thoughtful but not deeply probing** (e.g., no statistical treatment of measurement uncertainty), matching the descriptor for band 2.

Outcomes are evaluated via percentage accuracies and a list of methodological limitations, and suggestions for future work are offered (additional images, modelling solid angle).  This constitutes a **clear evaluation with implications**, albeit not comprehensive or rigorously quantified, so band 2 is the best fit.

The student reflects on rounding errors, image-resolution limits, assumptions about the phase of the moon, and alternative modelling opportunities.  These comments are **meaningful and threaded throughout** evaluation and conclusion, but they lack the depth and ongoing critical questioning expected for the highest band, placing them solidly in band 2.

The student employs correct notation for trigonometric functions, arcseconds ("), radians and degree symbols, and uses variables consistently (Î´, d, D, Î¸).  Minor slips appear (e.g., âsinÎ¸ = Î¸â typed instead of âââ in one heading, occasional mismatched parentheses), so full accuracy is not maintained throughout.  **Appropriate language is mostly consistent**, aligning with band 3.

A personal lens is woven through the narrative (motivation from smartphone moon photos, adaptation to telescope constraints, reflection on real-world use such as kite distance estimation).  These touches show a **clear personal approach**, but the investigation is not markedly innovative or unique in methodology; therefore band 2 is appropriate.

At 18 pages the write-up is generally concise, and almost all material supports the aim; however some repetition occurs (e.g., definition of arcseconds appears twice, lengthy quotations in the evaluation).  A few extended narrative sentences could be trimmed without loss of meaning.  Hence the work is **mostly relevant and reasonably succinct**, but not at the exemplary level required for band 4.

Mathematicsâsmall-angle trigonometric proofs, derivation of two distance formulas, Taylor-series justification, percentage-error analysis and real-data computationâis **highly relevant and used in a comprehensive manner** across the exploration.  The work goes beyond merely applying a formula; it builds, tests and validates that formula, justifying placement in band 5.

The student selects an original astronomical question, derives core results independently and chooses to collect personal telescope data rather than rely solely on textbook valuesâclear **evidence of independent decision-making**.  Nonetheless, the mathematics itself stays close to standard course techniques; there is limited extension beyond syllabus material, so the strand sits comfortably in band 2.

A detailed table of contents, numbered headings, and consistent subsections (Introduction, Small-angle Approximation, Angular Diameter, Application, Conclusion, Evaluation) give the report an **exceptionally clear structure**.  Figures, tables and formulae are labelled and cross-referenced; summary tables (e.g., Table 5) sit exactly where the reader expects them.  Introduction and conclusion are distinct and purposeful, confirming a level of organisation that meets the top-band expectation.

Content such as the Maclaurin expansion and error analysis sits at or slightly above SL level yet is handled correctly; all other mathematics (trigonometry, unit-conversion, ratio reasoning) is squarely within the AA SL syllabus.  The concepts are **well applied and interconnected**, meriting band 5 for level appropriateness.

annotations.comment: The studentâs pivot from a smartphone photo to MicroObservatory images demonstrates adaptability and personal engagement in overcoming methodological challenges.
The calculation of the mean pixel measurement using three trials shows sound statistical practice; include a standard deviation to quantify measurement variability.
The application of the general formula D=206265Ãd/(pxâangle) is correct and skilfully executed, demonstrating thorough understanding of unit conversion.
Table 5 effectively summarizes results, but the accuracy formula A=100â(Tâ0)/(TÃ100) is miswritten; correct it to A=100Ã|observedâtrue|/true or clarify the derivation.
The proposed extensions into angular area and parallax demonstrate substantial critical reflection and offer a valuable pathway for future investigation.
Student presents an informative and specific title that clearly indicates the focus and level of analysis, enhancing reader orientation.
The table of contents is comprehensive and accurately cross-referenced, demonstrating an exceptionally clear organizational structure.
The introduction richly sets context but contains lengthy sentences; consider trimming or splitting to improve conciseness without losing clarity.
The rationale thoroughly outlines the plan, but combining related aims into sub-paragraphs could improve readability and focus.
The first small-angle approximation is stated as âsinÎ¸ = Î¸â; for mathematical precision, replace â=â with âââ at the outset to reflect that this is an approximation.
The geometric diagram effectively supports the derivation of the approximation by clearly labeling triangles and aiding visual understanding of the relationship.
The inclusion of Desmos graphs with customized scales provides an effective visual representation of approximation accuracy, enhancing multiple representations.
The percentage-error calculation is correctly formulated, but clarifying the rounding rationale and potential propagation of rounding error could strengthen the analysis.
The cosÎ¸ values in Table 3 appear inconsistent for small Î¸ (e.g., cos0.01 â 1.00, not 0.100); verify your calculations and table entries for accuracy.
The Taylor-series explanation is well integrated, but consider stating it earlier or summarizing the series expansion to avoid lengthy in-text quotations.
The derivation of tan(Î´/2)=d/(2D) is clear; include a brief note on error introduced by small-angle substitution to demonstrate full understanding of approximation limits.
The general formula D=206265^n d/Î´ uses an exponent n without definition; clarify the role and value of n to avoid confusion in applications.
The derivation of the â57 ruleâ applies Ïâ3.14; reflect on the impact of this approximation on subsequent distance calculations and include an error estimate.
The conclusion succinctly revisits aims and methods, but slightly blends evaluation with summary; consider separating reflections on limitations into a distinct section for clarity.
The point on rounding is insightful, but using bold math notation for L^max in list text can distract; consider standard formatting for clarity.
The reflection on lunar phase timing shows critical awareness of observational limitations; deepening this by evaluating its quantitative effect would further strengthen the reflection.
The extensive reference list shows thorough research; ensure each source is cited correctly in-text to maintain consistency between in-text citations and references.
The source âSmall angle approximationsâ from Mathspanda may not meet academic credibility standards; consider prioritizing peer-reviewed or established academic sources.
Ensure consistency in bibliographic entries (e.g., uniform use of punctuation and access dates) to present a polished and professional reference list.
Consider alphabetically ordering web sources to improve retrieval and reader navigation through the references section.
